title: A Day as a Robotics Startup Founder
setting: Swoop Aero-inspired medical drone delivery startup, Melbourne VIC (co-working space and test facility)
companySize: Early-stage startup (18 employees — 10 engineers, 3 operations, 2 commercial, 2 regulatory, 1 founder/CEO)
description: Anika Patel is the founder and CEO of a robotics startup developing autonomous drones for medical supply delivery to remote and regional Australian communities. The company has completed proof-of-concept flights and is working toward CASA certification for beyond-visual-line-of-sight (BVLOS) operations. Anika's day spans engineering decisions, fundraising, regulatory navigation, customer conversations, and team management. She has a PhD in autonomous systems from the University of Sydney and three years of experience at a mining automation company before starting the venture.
salary: $120,000 - $180,000 AUD per year (founder salary, varies significantly with funding stage; equity is the real upside)

schedule.activity: Review overnight emails and news over coffee
schedule.note: A potential customer (remote health clinic in Far North Queensland) has responded to the proposal — they want a trial. Also, a competitor just announced a funding round.

schedule.activity: Engineering standup with the tech team
schedule.note: The perception team is debugging a LiDAR-based landing zone detection issue — the algorithm struggles with tall grass. The flight control team has a new wind disturbance rejection model ready for simulation testing.

schedule.activity: Deep work: review the CASA BVLOS application
schedule.note: The CASA application for BVLOS operations is the critical path item — without it, commercial operations cannot begin. Reviewing the safety case with the regulatory specialist.

schedule.activity: Board update call with lead investor
schedule.note: Monthly update: progress on CASA certification, customer pipeline, cash runway (8 months remaining), key hires needed (senior avionics engineer)

schedule.activity: Customer discovery call with a remote health service
schedule.note: Understanding the logistics challenges of delivering blood products, vaccines, and pathology samples to clinics 200+ km from the nearest hospital

schedule.activity: Work on the Series A pitch deck
schedule.note: Need to raise $5M to fund CASA certification, fleet manufacturing, and the first commercial deployments. Australian VCs want clear regulatory pathway and customer commitments.

schedule.activity: Lunch with the team at the co-working space cafe
schedule.note: Informal culture-building — a startup lives or dies by its team cohesion

schedule.activity: Technical deep-dive: simulation results for the new flight controller
schedule.note: Reviewing simulation data for the updated wind disturbance model — the drone needs to handle 35 km/h crosswinds for reliable outback operations

schedule.activity: Recruiting call with a potential senior hire
schedule.note: Interviewing a senior avionics engineer currently at Boeing Aerostructures Australia — excellent technical skills, needs to be convinced that a startup is worth the risk
Exploring a partnership with an established logistics provider for ground-side operations (loading, charging, maintenance) at delivery sites

schedule.activity: Flight test planning for next week
schedule.note: Planning a BVLOS demonstration flight at a rural test site near Bacchus Marsh — need CASA approval, landowner permission, and good weather
Three priorities for tomorrow: finalise the CASA application section on emergency procedures, send the Series A deck to two new VCs, close the senior hire

schedule.activity: Head home, but read a research paper on adaptive landing during dinner
schedule.note: The boundary between work and life is blurry at a startup — but the mission keeps you going

responsibilities: Set company vision, strategy, and priorities
Raise capital from investors (angels, VCs, government grants)
Navigate CASA regulatory requirements for drone operations
Recruit and retain top engineering talent in a competitive market
Maintain technical oversight of the autonomous systems architecture
Build customer relationships and close pilot agreements
Manage cash flow and make resource allocation decisions
Represent the company at industry events, conferences, and in media
challenges: Regulatory uncertainty — CASA certification timelines are difficult to predict and can make or break the business plan
Cash management — runway is finite, and fundraising takes longer than expected (6-9 months for a Series A round)
Recruiting against larger companies (Google, Boeing, mining companies) that offer higher salaries and more stability
Technical risk — autonomous flight in variable Australian weather conditions is genuinely difficult
Balancing the need to move fast with the need for safety in a system that flies over communities
Founder burnout — the emotional and time demands are relentless
rewards: Building something from nothing that could genuinely save lives in remote communities
The freedom to make decisions and see their impact immediately
Working with a small, passionate team where everyone's contribution is visible
The intellectual challenge of solving hard technical and business problems simultaneously
Potential for significant financial upside if the company succeeds
Being part of Australia's growing deep-tech startup ecosystem
careerPath: Founder/CEO through growth stages → potential exit (acquisition or IPO) or long-term company leadership
Alternative: after exit, become a venture partner at a deep-tech VC fund
Alternative: join a larger company in a senior leadership role (VP Engineering, CTO) with startup credibility
Alternative: serial entrepreneurship — apply lessons learned to the next venture
